closed_today.exit_rationale: PREEMPTIVE PRE-SL: 'SL 13400 just 0.2% below CMP 13426 — virtually guaranteed stopout on any morning weakness. Better to exit on auto-sector strength than to take the SL hit.' Thesis cited as INTACT (M&M Q4 beat, Maruti record April sales +33%).

PREEMPTIVE PRE-SL: 'SL 372 is just 1% below CMP and likely to trigger; better to exit on my terms now at -1.6% than get stopped at -2.1%.' Cited valuation/distribution concerns but those were knowable BEFORE entry on 05-05.

BINARY EVENT (Rule 11): FY26 audited results May 8 (T+2). Strategy itself flagged conf 0.62 < 0.70 threshold for event-trades during ENTRY at 10:17:06 ('CHANGING to NO_ACTION') — but order still went through; exited 30 min later for clean +0.2%. Rule 11 exit was correct given the entry was inadvertent.

PREEMPTIVE PRE-SL: 'P&L -2.7%, CMP 1436.80, SL 1420 only 1.2% away.' Reasoning explicitly acknowledged 'Q4 was actually GOOD (PAT +124% YoY, pre-sales +30%)' — i.e., thesis intact — but interpreted price fade as 'rejection signal'. Realty sector noise was knowable at entry.

PREEMPTIVE PRE-SL: 'CMP 206.48 sitting right at SL 206. Better to exit now at -1.2% than wait for SL trigger.' Position was inherited from Day-10 sequential round-trip; today's exit ~0.2% above stored SL means SL might have fired moments later — but exit was BEFORE SL, locking the loss.

PREEMPTIVE PRE-SL: 'CMP 3255 sitting just 0.3% above stored SL 3245. Faded sharply from 3315 high. Exiting near SL to avoid slippage on inevitable trigger.' Same-session round-trip (BUY 10:16, CLOSE 12:53 = ~157 min). Exit fill 3244.38 actually BELOW the stored SL 3245 — indistinguishable from letting SL fire, except locked at MARKET rather than triggered SL.

new_entries_open.note: TIGHT SL (-1.4%) for an Auto cyclical — wobble margin too small. Same-session round-trip exit at -1.4% confirms the SL placement was the issue, not the entry.
MIS intraday — status OPEN at EOD per DB; auto-cancel timing TBD
OK; reasoning text said SL 362 but field is 358 — minor reasoning-execution drift

ENTRY ERROR: own reasoning ended 'Rule 11 requires conf ≥0.70 for event trades within 3 sessions. CHANGING to NO_ACTION.' — but the BUY went through anyway. Exited 30 min later. Rule 11 self-detected but execution layer didn't honor the in-prose retraction.

DATA ERROR — SL 1265 is ABOVE entry 1259 on a long. Reasoning narrative said 'Risk 44 (3.5%)' implying SL≈1215 but executed field is 1265 (+6, wrong side). Same Rule 16 SL-DIRECTION failure mode as Day-11 NAVINFLUOR (SL=entry); this variant is SL > entry on a BUY = no downside protection AND structurally inverted. Rule 16 update from 05-05 review will only bite at next bot restart.
Reasoning text said SL 2980 but field is 2900 — wider than reasoned. Minor drift but on the safer side.
Smallest position 2% — anti-conviction-scaled (0.68 conf but smallest size). SL was 780 in field; reasoning said 790 — drift on the safer side.

patterns_found: PATTERN 1 — PRE-SL EXIT EPIDEMIC (the day's primary failure mode): 5 of 6 closes were preemptive market-closes on carry positions with thesis explicitly intact, citing proximity-to-SL as the rationale. MARUTI -₹1,007, BHEL -₹713, SOBHA -₹1,425, BANDHANBNK -₹816, M&M -₹1,140 = -₹5,101 combined. The reasoning template was identical across all five: 'CMP is X% away from SL → likely to trigger → better to exit at -A% than get stopped at -B%.' Each exit saved 0.3-1% vs the SL fire and surrendered 100% of recovery option. The SOBHA case is the most diagnostic — strategy explicitly stated 'Q4 was actually GOOD' (thesis intact) yet exited because chart faded. The M&M case is the most arithmetically clean — exit fill 3244.38 was BELOW stored SL 3245, so the strategy paid the SL price anyway and gave up the option that the SL might not have triggered cleanly. This is not a guardrail problem; this is a strategy-discipline problem.

PATTERN 2 — POST-CLUSTER ANCHORING FIX WORKED: Day-11 review's prose addition ('A morning admit cluster does NOT satisfy the session') produced the intended behavior change. 9 new BUYs distributed across 8 distinct decision cycles — TITAGARH 09:13, M&M 10:16, MARICO 10:16, HFCL 10:16, ABCAPITAL 10:16, CHOLAHLDNG 10:17, COFORGE 10:46, GRSE 11:46, CAMS 12:18. Compare Day-11: 3 admits in a single 22-second cluster at 11:13 then 257-min freeze. EOD deployment Day-11 48% → Day-12 73% (+25 pp). Activity level resolved; the active-trader experiment is now generating data on conviction-scaled execution rather than on whether the bot will trade at all.

PATTERN 3 — COFORGE SL-DIRECTION FAILURE (second occurrence in 2 sessions): COFORGE BUY at 10:46:22 submitted with stop_loss=1265 ABOVE entry=1259 — wrong side for a long position. Reasoning narrative claimed 'Risk 44 (3.5%) vs reward 71 (5.6%) = R:R 1:1.6' implying SL ≈ 1215. Executed SL field landed at 1265 (entry+6, wrong side), making the 'SL' functionally a near-entry trigger that cannot fire on downside. Engine emitted warning ('MODIFY sets SL above entry... locking in profit') but interpreted this as a beneficial trail-up rather than a structurally-inverted SL submission and DID NOT block. This is the same root cause as Day-11 NAVINFLUOR (stop_loss=entry=6900) — narrative-vs-executed-fields drift. The Rule 16 SL-DIRECTION CHECK addition from yesterday's review is forward-looking and only bites at next bot restart; it has not yet had a chance to prevent today's repeat.

PATTERN 4 — CHOLAHLDNG ENTRY-RETRACTION NOT HONORED: CHOLAHLDNG BUY at 10:17:06 — reasoning ended with the strategy's own self-correction: 'Confidence 0.62 — wait, Rule 11 requires conf ≥0.70 for event trades within 3 sessions. CHANGING to NO_ACTION.' But the order was still placed (CHOLAHLDNG 25@1730 logged as COMPLETE). Either (a) the JSON action field was BUY despite the reasoning self-retraction in prose, or (b) the engine ignored the 'NO_ACTION' phrase. Net cost: +₹73 round-trip (lucky-clean), but a pure decision-slot waste — and identical pattern to the Day-5 INDUSINDBK self-research-retraction failure mode where the in-prose self-correction never propagates to the execution.

PATTERN 5 — OVERRIDE STACK NON-BINDING ALL SESSION (correctly empty): risk_config.yaml overrides block has been {} since Day-11 EOD review. Risk monitor produced 14 ticks today, 7 of them HOLD (post-12:30) recapitulating 'every lever at base, nothing to loosen, nothing to tighten on shape.' Position sizing stayed 2-8.2% (cap 20%), max sector cap not approached, deployment 73% (cap 80%), no min-confidence binds, no risk-budget binds. The 8 BLOCKED rows in guardrail_log are entirely noise (4 conf=0.0 malformed payloads, 4 short-sell-after-CLOSE artefacts) — same noise classes the prior 2 reviews documented. ZERO real strategy proposals were declined by guardrails today. The day's losses were ALL inside the lane the guardrails permit.

PATTERN 6 — POSITION SIZING STILL UNEVEN (Day-10/11 carry observation): Today's 9 admits sized 2-8.2%, conviction range 0.62-0.70. Some conviction-scaling visible (M&M 0.70/8.2%, the largest size with the highest conviction), but counter-examples remain (CAMS 0.68/2.0% — second-highest conviction, smallest size; COFORGE 0.65/3.8% — mid-conviction, mid-small). The 'scale size with conviction' principle is biting probabilistically rather than systematically. This was a Day-10 observation, has persisted through Day-11 and Day-12, and is provisional (still not yet acted on per Day-10 review's 2-session evidence-window recommendation). Now that 3 sessions of data exist, a future TRADING PHILOSOPHY clause may be warranted — but not in this review (the pre-SL exit fix is the higher-leverage change for Day-13).

PATTERN 7 — BUYER OF NEW NAMES + SELLER OF EXISTING NAMES = NET NEGATIVE TODAY: Today's 9 admits contributed +₹8,664 unrealized lift; today's 5 carry-position pre-SL exits realized -₹5,029. Portfolio net change +₹3,635 (+0.36%). The strategy is admitting well (the Day-11 fix is producing winners-in-progress) but exiting badly (today's fix targets this). If the same 5 exits had been HOLDs through to actual SL or recovery, expected value would have been better given that the entry SLs were defensible (-2% to -4%, not pathological).

PATTERN 8 — SAME-SESSION ROUND-TRIPS (Rule 15 violations, 2 today): M&M (BUY 10:16, CLOSE 12:53 — 157 min, -₹1,140) and CHOLAHLDNG (BUY 10:17, CLOSE 10:46 — 30 min, +₹73). M&M's exit reasoning was the proximity-to-SL pattern (Pattern 1) on same-session, NOT Rule 15-style 'new fact post-entry' — the M&M Q4 print and Maruti April sales were both knowable at the 10:16 entry. CHOLAHLDNG's exit was Rule 11-correct (binary event) but the entry itself was a Rule 11 violation per the strategy's own prose self-correction. Both are entry-side discipline issues, not exit-side issues — the cleaner fix is to suppress the entry, not engineer around the exit.

guardrail_effectiveness.comment: Override stack stayed empty all session (correct per Day-11 review). 8 blocks were 100% noise. Today's losses were all inside the lane the engine permits — this is fundamentally a strategy-discipline issue, not a guardrail-tuning issue. Tightening guardrails in response to today's pre-SL exits would address a symptom, not the cause. The fix belongs in the prompt (Rule 17) where the exit decision is made.

deployment_assessment.comment: Major step up from Day-11 EOD 48% to Day-12 EOD 73%. Closes the 'capital under-deployed' opportunity-cost issue that the active-trader experiment was structured to surface. With cumulative -₹3,173 since inception (-0.32%) and 73% deployment, the experiment is now generating the right shape of data: actual trade outcomes at active-trader cadence, not flat-but-untraded-book cycles. Watch Day-13 for whether deployment can hold at 60-75% naturally without flipping back to under-deployment after the Pattern-1 fix takes effect (premise: if the bot is no longer dumping carry positions preemptively, it'll have less cash to redeploy and natural deployment will be slightly higher; same-day churn will drop).

activity_assessment.comment: Admit rate 62% — well above Day-10 baseline (46%) and 7.5x Day-11 (8%). The post-cluster anchoring fix from Day-11 review WORKED. No NO_ACTION-only afternoons. Activity is now firmly in the active-trader range; further nudges in this direction are not warranted until we see Day-13/Day-14 data.

changes_made.section: Rule 17 — PRE-SL EXIT DISCIPLINE — DO NOT FRONT-RUN YOUR OWN STOP-LOSS (NEW)
changes_made.what: Added new Rule 17 directly after Rule 16. Rule explicitly forbids EXIT rationales of the form 'CMP is close to SL — likely to trigger soon' on HELD positions (carry or current-session) with thesis intact. Mandates HOLD as the default, MODIFY-tighten-SL as the alternative, and EXIT only when a NEW post-entry fact has materialized. Calls out the specific anti-rationales seen today ('better to exit at -X% than get stopped at -Y%', 'CMP just above stored SL', 'Q4 was good but stock is fading', 'exit on sector strength before SL fires'). Cites all 5 Day-12 prior failure modes by name with explicit P&L numbers and reasoning quotes (MARUTI -₹1,007, BHEL -₹713, SOBHA -₹1,425, BANDHANBNK -₹816, M&M -₹1,140 = -₹5,101 combined).
changes_made.why: Five of six daily exits today fit the same pre-SL-preemption pattern; combined cost was -₹5,101, the worst single-day realized loss in the 12-session experiment. Each exit reasoning explicitly cited proximity-to-SL as the trigger; none cited new post-entry information. The SL placements at entry were defensible (-2% to -4%, within risk-budget). The asymmetry — saving 0.3-1% on the worst case while surrendering 100% of recovery option — is a measurable expectancy-negative pattern. Rule 15 (same-session round-trip discipline) only addresses the same-session subset of this failure mode; today's evidence is dominated by carry-position exits where the entry was 1+ sessions ago, which Rule 15 does not cover. Rule 17 closes that gap. The fix belongs in prose (the decision is made by the LLM), not in guardrails (which would require a per-symbol entry-vs-CMP-vs-SL geometry check that's complex to specify and would risk blocking legitimate Rule 11 / Rule 13 / Rule 17(c) exits).
changes_made.expected_impact: Forward-looking on next bot restart (system_prompt is built at boot from config.yaml + _TEMPLATE). Reduces probability of pre-SL preemptive exits on carry positions where the thesis is intact and the chart is wobbling toward SL. Day-13 watch metrics: (i) of all CLOSE/SELL transactions on carry positions, what fraction cite a post-entry NEW fact vs proximity-to-SL? Target: ≤30% are proximity-to-SL exits (vs today's 83%). (ii) Average realized loss per carry-position close: should DECREASE (paradoxically) as the strategy lets SLs fire at -2 to -4% rather than panic-closing at -1 to -3% — because more positions will recover before hitting SL at all, and the ones that do hit SL hit at the calibrated maximum, not at noise-level pre-emption. (iii) Total realized P&L for the next 5 sessions vs the prior 5 — premise is that the strategy's admit-side is healthy and the exit-side fix alone should flip session P&L from negative to flat-or-positive.

non_changes_with_rationale.decision: NO CHANGE — overrides block remains empty
non_changes_with_rationale.rationale: Today's losses were all inside the guardrail lane (0 real strategy blocks, 8 noise blocks). Tightening overrides (e.g., adding back min_sl_pct or default_sl_pct) would address a symptom, not the cause. The Day-12 failure was 5 voluntary market-closes on positions that had defensible SLs already in place — the SLs would have done their job had they been allowed to. Adding override constraints does not protect against voluntary exits; only the prompt does. Risk monitor's 7-tick HOLD pattern correctly reflects that nothing is binding.

min_sl_pct (0.005), max_sl_pct (0.05), default_sl_pct (0.02), max_position_pct (0.20), max_sector_pct (0.35), max_deployed_pct (0.80), min_confidence (0.50), min_risk_reward (1.5), daily_loss_limit_pct (0.075) are all working as intended. None bound today; today's losses were not caused by any of them being too lax.

recommendations: ENGINE-SIDE HARDENING (URGENT — second occurrence in 2 sessions): The guardrail engine should reject any BUY order where stop_loss >= price (for longs) or stop_loss <= price (for shorts), in addition to the existing min_sl_pct check. Today's COFORGE BUY landed with stop_loss=1265 above entry=1259 and the engine warned ('MODIFY sets SL above entry... locking in profit') but did NOT block — same as Day-11 NAVINFLUOR (stop_loss=entry=6900). The 'locking in profit' interpretation is correct for a TRAILED SL post-entry but WRONG for a fresh BUY at submission time. Engine needs to distinguish entry-time SL placement (must be on correct side of entry by ≥ min_sl_pct) from post-entry MODIFY (can move SL above entry to lock profit). This is a code change to src/trading/guardrails.py — outside the scope of strategy_review file edits but flagged for next code-side review. Rule 16's prose-side update from yesterday will help on next bot restart, but a defense-in-depth engine check is warranted.

MONITOR DAY-13 EXIT PROFILE: With Rule 17 in place after next bot restart, watch for: (a) of carry-position closes today (Day-13), what fraction cite proximity-to-SL vs post-entry NEW facts? Target: ≤30% are proximity exits. (b) Whether the strategy starts using MODIFY-tighten-SL as the legitimate middle-ground per Rule 17(b) — which would be a more sophisticated risk response than the current binary HOLD-or-CLOSE pattern. (c) Whether the trade count drops slightly (fewer churn closes) without deployment dropping (positions running their course rather than being exited and the cash redeployed).

CHOLAHLDNG ENTRY-RETRACTION HANDLING: The 10:17:06 CHOLAHLDNG BUY went through despite the reasoning prose ending with 'CHANGING to NO_ACTION.' This is a prose-vs-JSON-action drift — the JSON action field was BUY despite the prose self-retraction. Same root cause class as Rule 16 (R:R-claim-vs-executed-target mismatch) and the SL-direction failures (claimed SL vs executed SL). A general principle is starting to emerge: the prose narrative and the JSON action/numeric fields are sometimes incoherent. Worth considering an engine-side sanity check: if the reasoning string contains 'NO_ACTION', 'changing to no action', or similar self-retraction phrases AND the action is a non-trivial trade, log a warning. But this is fragile substring-matching; the better fix is an explicit prompt instruction that the JSON action field MUST equal the action proposed at the END of the reasoning, not anywhere earlier in the chain-of-thought. Hold this for a future review unless the pattern recurs.

POSITION SIZING HETEROGENEITY (third session of observation): Day-12 sized admits 2-8.2% across conviction 0.62-0.70 with no systematic conviction-scaling. CAMS at conf 0.68 sized 2.0% (smallest); M&M at 0.70 sized 8.2% (largest); the rest cluster at 4-5%. Day-10 review flagged this as provisional; Day-11 review held off on action; Day-12 makes it the third consecutive session. NOT changing this review (Rule 17 is the higher-leverage fix), but next review should consider a TRADING PHILOSOPHY clause: 'Conviction 0.70+ entries should be sized 7-10% (toward max_position_pct cap); 0.60-0.65 entries should be sized 3-5%. Uniform 5% sizing across conviction is uncalibrated.'

CONSIDER M&M-STYLE TIGHT SLs AS A SIZING ANTI-PATTERN: Today's M&M entry had SL only -1.4% from entry (3290 → 3245) on a Auto cyclical priced ₹3290 — that's an SL placed inside normal intraday wobble for a multi-thousand-rupee mid-volatility name. The same-session round-trip exit at -1.4% is unsurprising given the SL geometry. Rule 5 already mandates 'a large position with a wide SL is the failure-mode signature' but the inverse (a too-tight SL on a normally-volatile name) is also a failure mode. Worth considering a MIN_SL_FROM_VOLATILITY clause in a future review: 'For mid-volatility names (ATR 1.5-3% of price), the SL must be at least 1.5x the typical day's range.' Hold for now — too prescriptive for current state of evidence; Rule 17 may resolve this indirectly by suppressing the panic-close.

WATCH HFCL MIS AT EOD: HFCL BUY 600@133.5 (MIS LIMIT) status was OPEN at the bell. MIS auto-cancel timing (15:15-15:20) may or may not have flushed by 15:30. Check Day-13 pre-market whether HFCL has any residual MIS state — and if so, whether it became a CNC carry inadvertently. Not a strategy issue per se, but a state-management edge case worth confirming.
